definition: Central bank reaction function uncertainty is a distinct, additive source of volatility beyond standard macro uncertainty. FOMC communication shocks (reaction function news, distinct from economic outlook news) are empirically documented as major asset price drivers (Bauer-Lakdawala-Mueller 2022). QT creates an additional channel of discontinuous jump risk at unknown reserve scarcity thresholds.
evidence: Bianchi (2012): regime-switching monetary policy with hawkish/dovish shifts as significant vol source
Bauer-Lakdawala-Mueller (2022): FOMC communication shocks are major asset price drivers
Historical examples: 2021 transitory debacle, Dec 2018 dovish pivot, 2024 communication volatility
September 2019 repo spike and March 2023 banking stress demonstrate QT threshold risk
Won debate against endogeneity counter-argument for genuine discretionary policy instances
open_questions: Is separating reaction function uncertainty from standard macro uncertainty econometrically feasible?
Where are the current reserve scarcity thresholds for QT jump risk?
Is policy exogenous in normal times but endogenous near Minsky moments?
How does the ECB/BoJ dimension interact with the Fed channel?
